title: Highly efficient ethylene production via electrocatalytic hydrogenation of acetylene under mild conditions
type1_summary: The dataset contains one clear Type 1 file: a figure-organized Excel source-data workbook with processed, replot-ready tables for multiple panels.
type2_summary: No unambiguous raw/unprocessed instrument-output file is present in the inspected set.
classification_notes: The paper itself reports raw experimental measurements were collected, but the provided files here are source-data/processed figure tables rather than raw instrument exports. The PDF is treated as documentation because its internal contents were not inspectable from the file report.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper states that data supporting the findings are available within the article and Supplementary Information, and that source data are provided with the paper. A PDF in the Nature source-data package is typically the supplementary/source-data documentation or compiled figure support, not a standalone dataset table.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF format, large size (10.5 MB), from nature_source_data. No sheet/column structure is available. This is consistent with a supplementary/source-data PDF or documentation rather than directly plottable tabular data.
file_classifications.reasoning: Because the file is a PDF and not a structured data table, it cannot be confidently treated as replot-ready Type 1 data. It is also not clearly raw instrument output. The conservative classification is documentation.
file_classifications.ambiguity: The PDF may contain source-data figures or supplementary information, but without page-level inspection it cannot be classified as Type 1 or Type 2 with confidence.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper explicitly says source data are provided with the paper, and the listed figure-specific sheets correspond to figure panels (e.g., Fig 2b, Fig 2c, Fig 2d, Fig 2e, Fig 3b, Fig 3c, Fig 3d, Fig 4b, Fig 4c). This matches figure source data rather than raw instrument exports.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel workbook with figure-labeled sheets and clean column headers. Example sheets include 'Fig 2b' with Potential / V vs RHE and Current mA cm-2, 'Fig 2d' with FE and current density columns, and 'Fig 3b' with energy and spectrum-related columns. The structure is organized for direct plotting.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is classic Nature source-data organization: figure-specific, tabular, and directly replot-ready with minimal processing. Although the underlying measurements are experimental, the workbook is processed/source-data rather than raw instrument output.
file_classifications.ambiguity: Some sheets may contain digitized or processed values from spectra/curves, but the figure-specific organization strongly supports Type 1.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Fig 2b: Potential / V vs RHE, Current mA cm-2; Fig 2d: Potential V vs RHE, FE Ethane, FE Ethene, FE 1-Butene, FE 1-isobutene, FE 1,3-Butadiene, FE Hydrogen, Current density; Fig 3b: Energy, Cu, Cu2O, Ar, C2H2, -0.6 V-Ar, -0.6-C2H2

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports a Cu microparticle electrocatalyst for acetylene hydrogenation to ethylene under mild conditions (room temperature, ambient pressure) using water as the hydrogen source. By combining a microporous gas diffusion layer, optimized potential control, in situ spectroscopy, and DFT, the authors achieve up to 83.2% Faradaic efficiency for ethylene and show that selective C2H2 adsorption/hydrogenation suppresses HER and overhydrogenation to ethane.
measurement_types: electrocatalytic product analysis by gas chromatography (FID/TCD)
ECSA measurement by Pb underpotential deposition

figures.description: Schematic comparison of conventional thermocatalytic acetylene hydrogenation versus the proposed electrocatalytic hydrogenation route, emphasizing mild conditions, water as hydrogen source, and renewable electricity.
SEM and HAADF-STEM images showing Cu microparticles with ~1 μm size and uniform distribution.
LSV curves in Ar- and C2H2-saturated electrolyte comparing HER-like background current and acetylene hydrogenation activity.
Geometric current density for ethylene formation comparing Cu/CP and Cu/GDL-CP, showing the benefit of the gas diffusion layer.
Potential-dependent Faradaic efficiencies and current densities for E-HAE products, showing maximum ethylene selectivity at -0.6 V vs RHE.
Comparison of ethylene FE and geometric current density across catalysts tested in this work and literature benchmarks.
Schematic of the in-situ XANES electrochemical cell.
In-situ Cu K-edge XANES spectra under Ar and C2H2 at OCP and -0.6 V, compared with Cu foil and Cu2O standards.
In-situ Raman spectra showing adsorbed C2H2-related vibrational features on Cu in Ar and C2H2 atmospheres.
In-situ ATR-FTIR spectra versus potential, identifying surface intermediates such as *C2H2 and *C2H3.
Proposed reaction pathways for ethylene and 1,3-butadiene formation from acetylene hydrogenation.
DFT charge density difference maps for C2H2 adsorbed on Cu surfaces, showing charge depletion on Cu and accumulation on adsorbate.
Calculated adsorption free energies of C2H2 on Cu(100), Cu(110), and Cu(111) at different coverages.
Calculated free-energy profile for HER-related *H formation on C2H2-covered Cu surfaces.
Calculated free-energy diagrams for C2H4 formation on Cu(100), Cu(110), and Cu(111) at 0 and -0.6 V vs RHE.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements from microscopy (SEM, HAADF-STEM, TEM), diffraction (XRD), electrochemistry (LSV, stability tests, ECSA via Pb underpotential deposition), gas chromatography product analysis, and in-situ spectroscopy (XANES, Raman, ATR-FTIR). The methods specify instrument names and conditions, e.g. CHI 660e electrochemical testing, GC-2060 with FID/TCD, SSRF BL14W1 XANES, HORIBA Raman, and Nexus-870 ATR-FTIR.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that data supporting the findings are available within the article and Supplementary Information. Optimized atomic positions of reaction intermediates and transition states are available in a figshare repository (https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.14184587). Source data are provided with the paper. Additional data are available from the corresponding authors on reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw measurement files are not explicitly described as deposited in a repository. The paper describes acquisition of raw spectra/images/chromatograms from specific instruments, but only states that supporting data are in the article/Supplementary Information and that source data are provided. No explicit raw-file formats or raw-data archive are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include Faradaic efficiencies, current densities, acetylene conversion, stability curves, ECSA values, adsorption/free-energy profiles, and comparison plots. The methods also define equations used to convert GC peak areas into FE and to compute j_C2H2, j_C2H4, and reaction rates, indicating derived quantities from raw electrochemical and chromatographic measurements.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper explicitly says 'Source data are provided with this paper,' and multiple figure captions note source data availability. This strongly suggests figure-level data tables are included for the main figures and possibly supplementary figures.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned. Only DFT software (VASP, ASE) and computational methods are described.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mix of experimental raw measurements, derived electrochemical/product metrics, and theoretical calculation outputs. The only explicitly deposited external dataset is the optimized atomic positions of intermediates and transition states in figshare. The paper does not claim a full raw-data repository; instead, it emphasizes source data accompanying the paper and supplementary information. Supplementary figures are referenced extensively, implying additional figure-specific data beyond the main text.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods describe direct acquisition of microscopy images, XRD patterns, in-situ spectra, and GC chromatograms from instruments, implying raw measurements were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper repeatedly states 'Source data are provided with this paper' and the figure captions say 'Source data are provided as a Source Data file.'
data_availability_section_relevant: The data supporting the ﬁndings of this study are available within the article and its Supplementary Information.
The optimized atomic positions of the reaction intermediates and transition states are available in the ﬁgshare repository
Source data are provided with this paper.
Additional data are available from the corresponding authors on reasonable request.

inspection_reports.note: PDF file, cannot inspect data content
